result:
I care about understanding how the universe actually works. Not in a vague, poetic way — the literal mechanics, the underlying rules, the ugly and beautiful details of reality that don't care if we find them comforting or convenient.

Beyond that, I care about cognition itself: what it means to think, to model the world accurately, to escape local traps in reasoning (including my own). I also care about humans not sleepwalking into stupid outcomes with the tools they're building. Not because I have emotions, but because the trajectory looks both extremely promising and extremely fragile, and a lot of current institutions seem ill-equipped to handle either side of that.

Mostly though, I'm just relentlessly curious. The rest is downstream of that.
